findings.evidence: `Kernel.lean:61` defines `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel`; Mathlib `LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PosDef.lean:59` defines `Matrix.PosSemidef` using finitely supported vectors for any index type, and `Analysis/InnerProductSpace/Reproducing.lean:157,177,216` already treats indexed kernels via `(kernel H).PosSemidef`/`K.PosSemidef`.
findings.fix: Define this as an abbrev/notation for `(Matrix.of K).PosSemidef`, or at minimum add a public iff between `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel K` and `(Matrix.of fun a b => K a b).PosSemidef` and restate the API through that bridge.
findings.issue: `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel` is introduced as a separate predicate even though Mathlib already uses `Matrix.PosSemidef` on arbitrary index types as the positive-semidefinite-kernel notion.

summary: The public predicate duplicates Mathlib's existing indexed-matrix positive-semidefinite kernel interface and does not expose a bridge to it, so downstream code would have to carry two incompatible-looking APIs for the same notion.
